name: Nelson Riddle
description: American arranger, composer, bandleader and orchestrator (1921-1985)
abstract: Nelson Smock Riddle Jr. (June 1, 1921 – October 6, 1985) was an American arranger, composer, bandleader and orchestrator whose career stretched from the late 1940s to the mid-1980s.

concerts.label: Frank Sinatra and Lena Horne, Singers
Frank Sinatra and Lena Horne, Singers

works_created.t: Cross Country Suite: The Great Plains

about_quotes.speaker: Alan Broadbent
about_quotes.evidence: I was a pianist with Nelson Riddle for the last ten years of his life.
about_quotes.doc_title: Alan Broadbent interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, October 26, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Red Callender
about_quotes.evidence: Meanwhile I was doing things with Nelson Riddle and Billy May... I think it could have been... it was a concert with the Herman McCoy Group, Nelson Riddle, Buddy Collette and myself and June Christy were the featured artists on that.

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Buckingham,
about_quotes.evidence: Going back to when Sinatra started doing that stuff with Nelson Riddle in the 50s. Those were concept albums. Probably the first concept albums, those Sinatra albums with Nelson Riddle at Capitol.
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Buckingham, interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, February 25, 2019 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: DeFranco Buddy
about_quotes.evidence: I ran into Nelson, we got together for lunch... he took this assignment on... we made an LP and Nelson called it Cross Country Suite.
about_quotes.doc_title: DeFranco Buddy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Quincy Jones
about_quotes.evidence: I had just been through two months of re-orchestrating all Nelson Riddle’s arrangements ... for [Count] Basie’s band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Quincy Jones — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Edison
about_quotes.evidence: Then we became very close friends and he always wanted me on his albums.
about_quotes.doc_title: Harry Edison — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
